parsed_review.summary: CP00-194 (Risk B, 40 units, RP05.P08.M04.S20-RP05.P08.M06.S17) is a descriptor-only Matter Core permission/audit evidence terminal bridge. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the implementation is internally consistent and supports the pack's deferred readiness posture. Deliverable distribution (implementation:17, hermes_evidence:20, claude_review:2, test:1 = 40) and micro-phase/micro-title counts (M04:1, M05:22, M06:17; Secondary Workflow Slice:1, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:17) were independently re-derived from CP193_EVIDENCE_FULL_CONTROLS via CP193_DELIVERABLE_BY_CONTROL and reconcile exactly; they match the contract descriptor, registry requirements, validators, and the three dedicated tests in model.test.js. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: MATTER_CORE_CP194_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION sets all write/runtime/leak flags false (synthetic_only true); the service builder is a pure frozen-object function with no fs/db/network side effects; row-level flags (runtime_permission_evaluated, audit_event_written, dispatches_*_route, executes_rollback/retry, live_dom_rendered) are all false and replay_command_inert is true. Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic review packet/receipt labels (H05.CP00-194…) plus counts, with no_runtime_permission_evaluation/no_audit_event_write asserted — not product runtime. Authority boundary preserved: allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval and promotes_claude_to_final_approval false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation false, forbidden_review_evidence enumerates the full CP178+ rejection set. CP178+ hardening is correctly applied: the existing claude-review-result.json is a 0-byte-stdout placeholder explicitly labeled 'not valid review evidence' with status pending_hardened_review, and it blocks closeout rather than being accepted. No over-claim exists — manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication all honestly report production_ready:false/in_progress pending this review, final validation, and human closeout. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the 7 active matter refs from unrelated dirty/untracked files. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. This verdict is an independent read-only review recommendation; it does not constitute final approval, and human final approval for any enterprise trust claim remains required and preserved.
